events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to evaluate a scenario: "Closeout photos for EMT bending & installation show the installed work following the referenced detail. Clearances, labels, supports, and terminations shown for this element are in place, and the inspection card has no open correction note. Reference material on hand: NEC Art. 358, Ch. 9 (fill)."

We need to decide compliance. The scenario says the work follows the referenced detail, clearances, labels, supports, terminations are in place, inspection card has no open correction note. So it appears compliant. The stage is rough-complete. No component conditions flagged. So decision should be "pass". Risk low. Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 90%? But we can estimate. Since it's rough-complete, likely near 100% but not fully closed out. I'll put 90. Value: no numeric reading asked. Sound source: not asked. Confidence: high, say 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: none, so empty list. Actions: none. Rationale: brief explanation. References: NEC Art. 358, Ch. 9.

So JSON: decision "pass", risk "low", work_stage "rough-complete", component_conditions [], percent_complete 90, value 0, sound_source "", confidence 0.95, event_time 0, rate 0, order [], workable [], findings [], actions [], rationale "Installed EMT follows referenced detail, clearances, labels, supports, and terminations are in place, inspection card has no open correction note. Complies with NEC Art. 358 and Ch. 9 fill requirements.", references ["NEC Art. 358", "NEC Ch. 9"].

I'll output exactly that.

events.text:

  "decision": "pass",
  "risk": "low",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 90,

  "findings": [],
  "actions": [],
  "rationale": "Installed EMT follows referenced detail, clearances, labels, supports, and terminations are in place, inspection card has no open correction note. Complies with NEC Art. 358 and Ch. 9 fill requirements.",
  "references": ["NEC Art. 358", "NEC Ch. 9"]
}
